channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Remembering how 18F modernized government technology
item.description: Healthcare.gov, the government health insurance marketplace website, launched in October 2013 only to buckle under the weight of just 2,000 simultaneous users.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Don’t panic, gaming laptop buyers – Nvidia assures us that mobile RTX 5000 graphics cards won’t have the chip-level fault that hit desktop GPUs
item.description: The (rare) problem with missing rendering pipelines only applies to desktop graphics cards, not the laptop variety for RTX 5000.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: i2CAT promueve el primer laboratorio abierto europeo de investigación 6G en órbita baja
item.description: El centro de investigación i2CAT ha anunciado en el marco del Mobile World Congress la puesta en marcha del primer laboratorio en órbita terrestre baja de Europa (LEO, por sus siglas en inglés).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 8 obstacles women still face when seeking a leadership role in IT
item.description: If you are a tech leader, you might encourage your daughter to follow your path, imagining a journey, like yours, with challenges that can be overcome with hard work. But if you are a man — especially a white man — you are likely unaware of the massive obstacles she will face that you didn’t.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 20 organizations advancing women in tech
item.description: Despite national conversations about a lack of women in IT, women remain largely underrepresented in technology roles, according to a study by the National Science Foundation. And the pipeline doesn’t suggest a near-term correction.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: When to choose a bare-metal cloud
item.description: I did something last week that I had not done for a while. I recommended a bare-metal cloud service to a client. They had a particular purpose, and it was the right technology to solve the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vueling apuesta por la formación inmersiva de sus tripulantes de cabina
item.description: Lo avanzó Javier Álvarez, CIO de Vueling, en entrevista para CIO España: “La innovación forma parte del ADN de la compañía desde el inicio de los tiempos”. Y lo cierto es que la aerolínea de bajo coste no ha hecho sino confirmar estas palabras.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why weird JD Vance memes have taken over the internet
item.description: Ironically enough, a divisive moment in the Oval Office last weekend seems to have brought the entire internet together.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: How this sex-forward gay cruising site finally launched an Apple-approved iOS app
item.description: As an app designed to facilitate gay hookups, popular site Sniffies has had a limitation since it launched in 2018—it was accessible only via web browser. Until now. On Monday, the map-based cruising site debuted its Apple-approved iOS app.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: TSMC’s $100 billion U.S. commitment could calm Taiwan tensions
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here. TSMC’s new $100 billion U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Democrats ask for investigation into whether Musk is using government position to influence X advertisers
item.description: Democratic senators are asking the Department of Justice (DOJ) to investigate whether tech billionaire Elon Musk, a senior adviser to President Trump, is leveraging his government position to pressure advertisers into spending more on his social media platform, X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Senate Banking Committee Chair Tim Scott (R-S.C.) unveiled legislation Thursday taking aim at debanking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Micron just demoed the world's fastest SSD with PCIe 6.x tech, a sequential read speed of 27GB/s, and yes, it's just a prototype for now
item.description: Micron’s latest SSD nearly doubles PCIe 5.0 speeds, yet the ecosystem isn’t ready for mainstream deployment.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City, rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LibreOffice downloads on the rise as users look to avoid subscription costs
item.description: Interest in LibreOffice, the open-source alternative to Microsoft Office, is on the rise, with weekly downloads of its software package close to 1 million a week. That’s the highest download number since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Senate Democrat slams Trump's 'grift' meme coins
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) slammed President Trump for his launch of a meme coin in January, suggesting it might be the "biggest White House corruption in a century.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: This web hosting platform elevates your online presence
item.description: With unmatched performance, bandwidth, and security, WordPress.com offers everything you need for a successful website.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Majority of voters don't want government to make decisions using AI: Poll
item.description: A majority of U.S. voters do not want the government using artificial intelligence (AI) to make decisions, according to a new poll from Americans for Responsible Innovation (ARI) shared first with The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Had Spotify problems recently? It's clamped down on Premium APK 'modded' apps – here's what's happening
item.description: Spotify appears to have run out of patience with cracked apps, but it should be normal service for everyone else.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amd's FSR 4 Dethrones Fsr 3 and Nvidia's DLSS CNN Model: A Game-Changer in Midrange GPU Market?
item.description: AMD's new Radeon RX 9070 series GPUs launch with a strong midrange focus, while the improved FSR 4 upscaling method dethrones Nvidia's former DLSS CNN model and rivals performance mode.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tres requisitos imprescindibles a la hora de contratar a proveedores de IA
item.description: Dos años de experimentación con la IA generativa pueden haber dado lugar a varios casos de uso valiosos pero, en este período, los líderes de TI también han aprendido que no hay que lanzarse a ciegas a esta nueva tecnología en rápida evolución.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Utah just passed the country’s first age-verification bill for app stores. But the fight isn’t over
item.description: On Wednesday, Utah became the first state in the country to pass legislation mandating that app stores verify users’ ages and get parental consent for certain activity on minors’ accounts.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: Utah has become the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This shaving brand was banned on TikTok, so it started an OnlyFans
item.description: How can a razor brand market its products online when the word razor is shadow banned? Start an OnlyFans, of course. London-based Nimbi, which launched at Erewhon in January 2024, began posting on TikTok last summer but noticed its views were disappointingly low.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Asymmetric AI warfare in the era of cheap intelligence
item.description: Artificial intelligence innovation is at the forefront of this advancement and presents the necessity to detect emerging threats. The post Asymmetric AI warfare in the era of cheap intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pentagon signs AI deal to aid military decision-making
item.description: The Department of Defense has signed a contract with start-up Scale AI to use artificial intelligence for military planning and operations, marking the Pentagon's latest incorporation of emerging tech into its workflows.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DOJ charges Chinese hackers, government officials in campaign targeting US agencies
item.description: The U.S. government has charged 10 Chinese hackers-for-hire and two of the country's security officials in a scheme to steal data from American entities and dissidents of the Chinese government, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salesforce’s Agentforce 2dx update aims to simplify AI agent development, deployment
item.description: Salesforce on Wednesday released the third version of its agentic AI offering — Agentforce 2dx — to simplify the development, testing, and deployment of AI agents that can automate business processes across departments, such as sales, service, marketing, finance, HR, and operations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top data storage certifications to sharpen your skills
item.description: Enterprise data storage skills are in demand, and that means storage certifications can be more valuable to organizations looking for people with those qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
